summary.intent: Implement a ToggleGroup component for the ars-ui library, open a draft PR (#670), and shepherd it through automated Codex review by addressing all review threads.
summary.outcome: PR #670 was opened, Codex review completed with 5 threads, all issues were remediated (invalid ARIA attributes, missing type="button", disabled reset logic, idle focus seed order), tests/snapshots/spec passed, and fixes were committed and pushed.

repo: Codex review is triggered by commenting '@codex review' on a PR and polled via GraphQL reviewThreads; 👀 reaction signals active review, threads appear when issues are found.
aria-orientation and aria-required are only valid on role='radiogroup', not role='group' — ToggleGroup emits these conditionally based on role.
Items in ToggleGroup require explicit type="button" to prevent implicit form submission.
Idle focus seeding must follow DOM registration order, not key-sort order.
Disabled state should not block form reset — reset and disabled are orthogonal concerns.
Snapshot tests use INSTA_UPDATE=always to accept new/changed snapshots; cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject must be run without a test filter to avoid false positives.
Spec files in spec/components/utility/ are validated with 'cargo xtask spec validate' and have a TOC generated via 'cargo xtask spec toc'.
PRs with insta snapshots require the 'snapshot-reviewed' label.
Nightly fmt (cargo +nightly fmt --all) is required before commit; regular fmt may miss some formatting issues.

code.finding: aria-orientation and aria-required emission gated to radiogroup role only
type="button" added to item attribute output
idle focus seed changed from key-sort to registration order; disabled no longer blocks form reset

workflow: Polling Codex review via GraphQL with a 2-minute sleep loop is the standard pattern; ~6-10 minutes is normal for larger diffs.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject -p <crate> --features i18n --lib (without test filter) is the correct full snapshot validation command.
Running 'gh pr checks 670 --watch --fail-fast' in parallel with Codex polling provides CI visibility without blocking.
Applying patches and running targeted tests before updating snapshots avoids noise in snapshot diffs.

friction: Codex review took ~10+ minutes with multiple 2-minute polling cycles before threads appeared.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject cannot be combined with a narrow test filter — it incorrectly flags all other snapshots as unreferenced, requiring a full library run.
cargo +nightly fmt caught formatting issues that the standard formatter missed, requiring an extra fix-and-recheck cycle.
open_items: PR #670 is still in draft; it needs CI to fully complete (Coverage, Dioxus feature flags, Release Verification were still running at commit time) before it can be marked ready for review.
Codex may re-review after the remediation push — thread resolution loop may need another pass.
